root_text.an3.23:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an3.23:1.4: So sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā sabyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.23:1.5: Tamenaṁ sabyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ sabyābajjhā phassā phusanti.
root_text.an3.23:1.6: So sabyābajjhehi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno sabyābajjhaṁ vedanaṁ vedayati ekantadukkhaṁ, seyyathāpi sattā nerayikā.
root_text.an3.23:2.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, abyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, abyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an3.23:2.2: So abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, abyābajjhaṁ vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, abyābajjhaṁ manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā abyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.23:2.3: Tamenaṁ abyābajjhaṁ lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ abyābajjhā phassā phusanti.
root_text.an3.23:2.4: So abyābajjhehi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno abyābajjhaṁ vedanaṁ vedayati ekantasukhaṁ, seyyathāpi devā subhakiṇhā.
root_text.an3.23:3.1: Idha pana, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti.
root_text.an3.23:3.2: So sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vacīsaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā, sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi manosaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharitvā sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi lokaṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.23:3.3: Tamenaṁ sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi lokaṁ upapannaṁ samānaṁ sabyābajjhāpi abyābajjhāpi phassā phusanti.
root_text.an3.23:3.4: So sabyābajjhehipi abyābajjhehipi phassehi phuṭṭho samāno sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi vedanaṁ vedayati vokiṇṇasukhadukkhaṁ, seyyathāpi manussā ekacce ca devā ekacce ca vinipātikā.
root_text.an3.23:3.5: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an3.23:1.1: “These three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.23:1.3: Firstly, a certain individual makes hurtful choices by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.23:1.4: Having made these choices, they’re reborn in a hurtful world,
translation_text.an3.23:1.6: Touched by hurtful contacts, they experience hurtful feelings that are exclusively painful—like the beings in hell.
translation_text.an3.23:2.1: Furthermore, another individual makes pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.23:2.2: Having made these choices, they are reborn in a pleasing world,
translation_text.an3.23:2.4: Touched by pleasing contacts, they experience pleasing feelings of perfect happiness—like the gods of universal beauty.
translation_text.an3.23:3.1: Furthermore, another individual makes both hurtful and pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind.
translation_text.an3.23:3.2: Having made these choices, they are reborn in a world that is both hurtful and pleasing,
translation_text.an3.23:3.3: where hurtful and pleasing contacts strike them.
translation_text.an3.23:3.4: Touched by both hurtful and pleasing contacts, they experience both hurtful and pleasing feelings that are a mixture of pleasure and pain—like humans, some gods, and some beings in the underworld.
translation_text.an3.23:3.5: These are the three individuals found in the world.”
